hehe I go to zone into DC with my 13 cleric, when who should start beating on me, but a light blue Corflunk. After running around with his head for a few days, I resist the urge to destroy it in a fit of frustration. Ding 14, running to grab my spells, and what do I see before me? Zarchroomi(sp?) standing on a hill. HA! and just my luck, I get the helm first try...w00t, no more small cloth cap! =D

pp per AC?
QuoteReply
# May 25 2001 at 3:11 PM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
whats the price for BF armor anyways?

boots sell for 40 and 5 ac, bracers have same ac but only 33pp?

helmet is special, i know, but what about the rest?

8pp per AC?
▲ Page top
RE: pp per AC?
QuoteReply
# Jan 16 2003 at 10:02 PM Rating: Decent
animekenji
1,123 posts
The Bloodforge parts have the same AC as small ringmail but are a lot heavier and have more restrictions on who can wear them, which limits your market. I would start with whatever small ringmail parts go for on your server and deduct for the added weight and restrictions on who can equip. The helm with the buffs is special, so you would add for the buffs over small ringmail.

That's speaking from a strictly practical viewpoint. The full suit looks pretty cool, so I suppose there are a few people willing to pay for the chance to strike a pose in this stuff, I'm just not one of them. ;)
____________________________
If you were me and I were you, would we still be friends?

# Nov 27 2019 at 11:09 AM Rating: Decent
animekenji
1,123 posts
It's because the Bloodforge Brigade has a longer and more complex history of being enemies to the ogres than the gnomes do and they only allow dwarves to join.

▲ Page top
Luck or what?
QuoteReply
# Mar 04 2001 at 5:38 AM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1593374866390
8 posts
My dwarf cleric did this quest, over a period of months, collected both heads as he wandered about, had them banked for a while before discovering what to do with them. It was done without a lot of hope for anything much, having got the gauntlets in trade and ended up selling them for 5pp. Imagine my delight when on receipt of the heads Mr Bloodforge handed me this little beauty. The bronze helm hit the trash faster than a greyhound after a rabbit.

I do wonder how many times some people did this quest specifically for the helm, and got something decidedly less choice?

Quest completed by Grimstone of Morell-Thule.
▲ Page top
RE: Luck or what?
QuoteReply
# Mar 12 2001 at 11:25 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1593361318888
Scholar
30 posts
I received the Helm the first...and only...time I have completed this quest to date. I sold the Helm in Freeport market for 600pp as I am a Druid and cannot wear.However it did take me three weeks to get both Ogre heads, Zarchoomi seems uncommonly rare. Also I understand that the Helm is rarely given...so...if you are a Dwarf it is an excellent item, if not the pay return was nice, for me anyway. Good luck.
▲ Page top

Price is Incorrect
QuoteReply
# Mar 19 2001 at 10:04 AM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
It is no better than a Blackened Iron Crown (and I beleive it looks teh same) and I bought a BI crown for 90 plat the other day. The helm is worth no more than 90 plat tops. It drops common in Runneyeye off a 25 mob
▲ Page top
Price is Incorrect
QuoteReply
# Nov 29 2019 at 6:39 PM Rating: Decent
animekenji
1,123 posts
Anonymous wrote:
It is no better than a Blackened Iron Crown (and I beleive it looks teh same) and I bought a BI crown for 90 plat the other day. The helm is worth no more than 90 plat tops. It drops common in Runneyeye off a 25 mob


It's similar, but the stats are a little lower. Corflunk is only 10-12 and Zarchoomi is 10-14. They are on a spawn cycle, but it's easy enough to figure out. Just keep killing any goblin that is wandering around away from the camps. When you see a goblin wizard, that is Corflunk's placeholder. A goblin shaman is Zarchoomi's. So this quest can be done at a lower level than Runnyeye, and it can be solo'd. Running around alone in Runnyeye when there are still a lot of dark blue and higher mobs up usually doesn't end well. Also, if you're high enough to kill both of them, then you are also high enough to be able to kill the occasional greenblood that might wander by, which are the biggest threat, unlike inside Runnyeye where there are still going to be a few mobs that can rip you apart if they come into aggro range. In Butcherblock, if you do get into trouble, you make a beeline straight for the nearest guard. You don't have that option inside Runnyeye. You have to follow the tunnels to one of the zonelines, drawing aggro from every mob you pass along the way. Given a choice between doing the easy Ogre Heads quest for a slightly inferior suit of armor, or going inside Runnyeye looking for Blackened Alloy parts, I'd rather do Ogre Heads now and save Runnyeye for later.
____________________________
If you were me and I were you, would we still be friends?
▲ Page top
RE: Price is Incorrect
QuoteReply
# Mar 20 2001 at 2:54 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1593361318888
Scholar
30 posts
Consider this...the Spawn rate on the Ogres are few and far in between...also the reward for the heads is one piece of armor at a time, and the Helm is a rare gift. I was paid 600pp for this item on Drinal. I don't think it is worth that...maybe 150 to 200pp is a fair price. But it is relative, anything is worth what someone will pay for it.
